metadata.description: Category/discovery shopping workflow. Handles broad exploratory queries like 'good coffee machine' or 'gaming laptop under 30k' — parses category + constraints, clarifies when too vague, searches the category across 16 e-commerce sites + 4 community forums, analyzes value/risks across options, and synthesizes a tiered recommendation (best overall / budget pick / alternatives). For named specific products (brand+model), the product_research workflow is used instead.

phases.name: Understand Query
phases.goal: Parse intent, extract constraints, check if clarification is needed.

phases.name: Clarify If Needed
phases.goal: Ask clarifying questions if query is too vague, otherwise pass through.

phases.name: Search & Collect Reviews
phases.goal: Run shopping_search, then fetch detailed reviews for top products.

phases.name: Analyze Value & Risks
phases.goal: Score deals, flag review red flags, give buy/wait timing advice.

phases.name: Synthesize Recommendation
phases.goal: Build tiered recommendation: Best Overall, Budget Pick, alternatives.

phases.name: Format & Deliver
phases.goal: Format recommendation as a polished Telegram-ready message.

steps.name: understand_query_check_clarity
steps.title: Understand Query & Check Clarity
steps.instruction: Read the user's query — this workflow handles category/discovery queries (specific product lookups are routed to product_research). Extract: category or product-type, budget if mentioned, key use case, brand preferences if narrow. Check user profile for past preferences. If the query has no category AND no use case AND no constraint, set needs_clarification=true and list 1-3 specific questions (budget? use case? must-have feature?). Otherwise set needs_clarification=false.
steps.done_when: parsed_intent has product/category/constraints extracted, or needs_clarification is set.

steps.name: ask_clarifying_questions
steps.title: Ask Clarifying Questions
steps.instruction:
Read parsed_intent and user_query. This workflow handles CATEGORY/DISCOVERY queries — the user named a category or broad need, not a specific brand+model. Decide: does the query have enough constraints (budget, use case, key feature) to shortlist candidates, or should we ask 2-3 questions first?

HAS ENOUGH: e.g. 'coffee machine under 5000 TL for a household of 2', 'gaming laptop for League of Legends on 1080p'. Return the query as clarified_query immediately.

NEEDS CLARIFICATION: generic category with no constraints ('coffee machine', '3d printers', 'laptop'). Ask 2-3 tight questions about budget, use case, and one must-have constraint. Use the needs_clarification response to pause.
steps.done_when: clarified_query is ready for search — either the original specific query or the user's answers incorporated.

steps.name: search_and_collect_reviews
steps.title: Search & Collect Reviews
steps.instruction: Call shopping_search with the query from clarified_query (or user_query if clarification was skipped). The tool returns products from 16 e-commerce sites + community data from 4 forums. Then for the top 2-3 product URLs in the results, call shopping_fetch_reviews to get detailed reviews. If shopping_search returned few/no products, use web_search as a supplement for manufacturer specs or international retailers. Combine everything into search_results (products + community) and review_data (detailed reviews from URLs). Do NOT fabricate any data.
steps.done_when: search_results has products with real prices from the tool, review_data has reviews from at least 1 source.

steps.name: analyze_value_and_risks
steps.title: Analyze Value & Risks
steps.instruction: Read search_results and review_data. This is a category-discovery analysis: compare candidates against each other on value (price-to-performance), coverage of the user's use case, and risk (recurring complaints on Şikayetvar, thin review counts). Do NOT frame as buy-or-wait for a specific SKU — frame as which segment of the category matches the user best. Use shopping_compare for value scoring across the shortlist. Produce deal_analysis with: per-candidate value verdict, segment recommendations, and red flags to avoid.
steps.done_when: deal_analysis covers value scoring, risk flags, and buy/wait timing.

steps.name: synthesize_recommendation
steps.title: Synthesize Recommendation
steps.instruction: Read all artifacts. Build a tiered CATEGORY recommendation: Best Overall (best value across the shortlist matching the use case), Budget Pick (cheapest that still meets the key requirement), and 1-2 alternatives from adjacent segments. Use shopping_alternatives if a different brand family or previous-gen meets the need at lower cost. Include warnings from deal_analysis. Structure as clear readable text with category rationale, product names, prices, sources, and when each pick fits best.
steps.done_when: recommendation has top pick, budget option, and timing advice.

steps.name: format_and_deliver
steps.title: Format & Deliver
steps.instruction: Take the recommendation artifact and format it for Telegram delivery. Lead with a one-line answer to the user's original query. Present top pick with price and where to buy. Then budget option if different. Then alternatives. Then warnings. Then timing advice. Keep it scannable — use bold for product names and prices, bullet points for comparisons. End with 'Watch price' / 'Compare more' / 'Ask me' action hints.
steps.done_when: shopping_response is a polished, user-facing Telegram message.
